Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-03-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Mills (appearing by Court Call) for Kurt Kaupke. James Henneffer for Karen Kaupke.

The Court states that it is prepared to grant the motion and asked Mr. Mills for Mr. Kaupke's current address. The Court is concerned that Mr. Kaupke is not receiving proper notice of the May 3, 2013 trial date. Mr. Mills reported that he believes that Mr. Kaupke is back in the United States, that he confirms that Aliso Viejo address and that he will forward Mr. Kaupke's email address to Mr. Henneffer. The Court grants the motion to be relieved as counsel (subject to review of order). The Court sets aside May 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept 204 for trial if needed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Off Calendar Oct-02-2012 Continued To Feb-07-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: James Hennefer for Karen Kaupke (Trustee). Charles Mills appeared by CourtCall for Kurt Kaupke (Petitioner).

Mr. Hennefer reported that Kurt Kaupke has not release any documents and that he was unable to take his deposition. Mr. Mills stated that his client is ill and that he is doing everything that he can to get the documents from his client. Mr. Hennefer stated that Kurt Kaupke has violated the Stipulation and Order and that he will seek appropriate remedy. Mr. Mills stated the trial will take two one-half day. The Court set trial dates on February 7, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. and February 8, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Off Calendar Aug-28-2012 Continued To Oct-02-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicolet Corliss for Karen Kaupke. Charles Mills appeared by telephone for Kurt Kaupke.

Ms. Corliss stated that she has heard nothing from Mr. Mills or his Client. Mr. Mills explained that his Client is very ill, stated that he thinks his Client will be able to travel within the next few weeks and that he was ameenable to paying/sharing airfare and hotel expenses for Ms. Corliss to travel to them for the purpose of taking Mr. Kaupke's deposition. Counsel are to work out the expenses. The Petition for First Formal Accounting of Trustee, etc. was continued to October 2, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the deposition and for setting of trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Counsel stipulated to the following: Kurt Kaupke will appear in San Francisco for a deposition in July and documents will be provided to Mr. Hennefer one week before the deposition date. Counsel will work out the specific date. Mr. Hennefer will draft a Stipulation and Order reflecting the above. Objections to the accounting, the issue of removal and the issue of surcharge are still pending. Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Hennefer for Karen Kaupke (Trustee). John Lister for Karen Casey (Judgment Creditor). Charles Mills appeared by telephone for Kurt Kaupke.

Mr. Hennefer stated that depositions were not completed. Mr. Hennefer will file a Motion to Compel Discovery. Discussions were held regarding venue of the Petition of Judgment Creditor Karen Casey for Order That Trustee Satisfy All or Part of Judgment and oral argument was also presented. The Court granted the Petition of Judgment Creditor Karen Casey for Order That Trustee Satisfy All or Part of Judgment (subject to receipt of an order). Mr. Lister will prepare the order. The Court denied Mr. Mills request for a statement of decision. The Petition for Formal Accounting of Trustee, etc. was continued to May 8, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Oct-26-2011 Continued To Jan-04-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gerhard Stoll for Charlene Kaupke.

No appearance was made by Attorney Charles Mills. Mr. Stoll stated that discovery has gone no where and that opposing Counsel has not returned his calls. The Petition for First Formal Accounting of Trustee, etc. was continued to January 4, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status. The ex parte petition was taken off calendar. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Charles Mills to appear on January 4, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the underlying petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Off Calendar Jul-20-2011 Continued To Oct-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Mills appeared by telephone for Kurt Kaupke. James Hennefer for Karen Kaupke.

Mr. Hennefer stated that the Parties are light years apart. Mr. Mills stated that they need to exchange documents, that they need to take depositions and that his Client was ill. The matter was continued to October 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery. Mr. Hennefer stated that the Trustee has complied with not taking any funds from the Sonoma property and will continue to do so through the trial. The Court stated that it will modify the order to reflect "until further order of the Court" provided the stipulation remains in effect.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Kaupke
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Continued To Mar-09-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Hennefer for and with Karen Kaupke. Charles Mills for and with Kurt Kaupke.

Mr. Hennefer stated that he filed a response to the new objection and the petitions today, that they restored the Trust account to the status it was and that they will not use anymore Trust funds for the Kenwood property. Mr. Mills stated that the petition to remove Trustee was formally withdrawn and that an accounting had been provided. The Parties stipulated to continuing the restraining order to the next hearing date. The Parties aslo agreed to participate in mediation (order for the same signed in open Court). Mediation is to be completed by March 2, 2011. Counsel is to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 24, 2011. Status of mediation is also set for March 2, 2011 at 9;00 a.m, in Dept. 204 and todays matters were continued to March 9, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Remove Trustee; To Appoint Successor Trustee; For Order Compelling Turn Ove Rof Trust Document, Accounting And Payment Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Off Calendar

Petition To Remove Trustee; To Appoint Successor Trustee; For Order Compelling Turn Ove Rof Trust Document, Accounting And Payment Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Jul-07-2010 Continued To Sep-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Hennefer for and with Karen Kaupke (Trustee/Respondent). Charles Mills appeared by telephone for Kurt Kaupke.

Mr. Hennefer stated that he was in the process of preparing an informal accounting, that he hired Lynn Nightengale (Probate Paralegal) to prepare the accounting but that they were missing a file containing Kurt Kaupke's obligations and payments to the Trust which were turned over to Kurt in 2007, that they did not receive it back and that 60 days was reasonable to have the accounting completed. Mr. Mills stated that his Client does not have said file, that it is the duty of the Trustee to keep records and that Counsel could prepare a request to produce documents. Mr. Mills stated that the petition was verified by Mr. Kaupke and that he witnessed Mr. Kaupke's signature. The Court stated that it wanted to see the petition verified and requested that one page be signed by Mr. Kaupke and one page signed by Mr. Mills. Mr. Mills will have said verifications to the Court by next week. The matter was continued to September 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for receipt of the verifications to the petition and for status of discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
